sourceTitle: Organize your everyday life with Notion AI

questions.question: What is the first step to start using Notion AI in a Notion page?

options.text: Click the "AI" button in the top toolbar
options.explanation: There is no dedicated AI button in the top toolbar; Notion AI is invoked from the page content.

options.text: Go to a new line and press the space key
options.explanation: The guide says to start Notion AI by creating a new line and pressing the space key, which opens the prompt field.

options.text: Open the Settings menu and enable AI
options.explanation: AI is already built into the workspace; you don’t need to enable it from Settings.

options.text: Install a third‑party AI extension from the marketplace
options.explanation: Notion AI is native to Notion and doesn’t require external extensions.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT listed as a capability of Notion AI?

options.text: Summarizing long articles
options.explanation: Summarizing is explicitly mentioned as a function.

options.text: Translating text into other languages
options.explanation: Translation is listed among the capabilities.

options.text: Generating video content
options.explanation: The guide never mentions video generation; it focuses on text‑based tasks.

options.text: Extracting themes and action items from notes
options.explanation: Extracting themes/action items is a highlighted use case.

questions.question: What are the three essential components of a good Notion AI prompt?

options.text: Type of content, topic, desired format
options.explanation: The guide lists these three components as key to effective prompting.

options.text: Length of output, tone, keyword density
options.explanation: While tone and length matter, they are not the three core components outlined.

options.text: User’s mood, time of day, device type
options.explanation: These factors are irrelevant to the prompt structure described.

options.text: Number of bullet points, font style, color scheme
options.explanation: Formatting of the page itself is not part of the prompt guidance.

questions.question: When using a custom AI block for weekly meal planning, how can you ensure the AI only suggests recipes that fit your dietary constraints?

options.text: Add a line in the block that lists the ingredients you have and specify any restrictions (e.g., no nuts)
options.explanation: The guide advises including specific instructions like “no nuts” and listing available ingredients.

options.text: Enable the ‘Dietary Filter’ toggle in the Notion settings
options.explanation: There is no such toggle mentioned; constraints are set via the prompt.

options.text: Upload a PDF of your dietary guidelines for the AI to read
options.explanation: The guide does not describe uploading external files as a way to constrain AI output.

options.text: Ask the AI to generate a random list of meals and then manually discard the ones you don’t like
options.explanation: While possible, it defeats the purpose of prompting the AI with clear constraints.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the thumbs‑up and thumbs‑down buttons after an AI response?

options.text: To let you vote on the aesthetic design of the Notion UI
options.explanation: The buttons are not related to UI design.

options.text: To report the quality of the AI’s answer, helping engineers improve the model
options.explanation: The guide states that feedback via these buttons helps improve the technology.

options.text: To lock the AI response so it can’t be edited later
options.explanation: Feedback does not affect editability of the response.

options.text: To automatically copy the response to your clipboard
options.explanation: Copying is done with standard shortcuts, not the feedback buttons.

questions.question: Which of the following best describes how Notion AI learns over time?

options.text: It constantly pulls new internet data to update its knowledge base in real time
options.explanation: Notion AI does not scrape the web in real time; it relies on pre‑trained models.

options.text: It iterates its model by testing against known answers using test data and incorporates more training data from user inputs
options.explanation: The article explains the three‑step process of training data, test data, and iterative learning.

options.text: It memorizes each individual user’s previous prompts and reuses them verbatim in future responses
options.explanation: AI does not store prompts verbatim; it uses context but does not copy past prompts.

options.text: It relies on a fixed rule‑based engine that never changes after initial release
options.explanation: The guide emphasizes that AI improves over time, not that it stays static.

questions.question: If you want Notion AI to generate an action plan for a goal you set, which block type would you most likely use?

options.text: Summary block
options.explanation: Summary blocks condense existing content; they don’t create new action plans.

options.text: Custom AI block
options.explanation: Custom AI blocks let you specify prompts such as “create an action plan for X.”

options.text: Database view block
options.explanation: Database views display data but don’t generate new content.

options.text: Inline equation block
options.explanation: Equation blocks are for math, not for generating action plans.

questions.question: Which scenario demonstrates the most effective use of Notion AI’s “Improve your writing and grammar” feature?

options.text: You paste a raw transcript of a meeting and ask AI to make it sound more formal
options.explanation: The feature is designed to polish existing text, such as making a transcript formal.

options.text: You ask AI to create a new logo design for your brand
options.explanation: Logo design is visual, outside the scope of the text‑focused AI.

options.text: You request AI to calculate the budget for a project
options.explanation: Budget calculations are numeric tasks, not writing improvement.

options.text: You use AI to set up a recurring reminder in Notion
options.explanation: Reminders are a built‑in Notion feature, not a writing‑enhancement use case.

questions.question: What is a potential drawback of over‑engineering your prompts, according to the guide?

options.text: The AI will refuse to respond because the prompt is too complex
options.explanation: The guide doesn’t say the AI will refuse; it may just produce less useful output.

options.text: It defeats the purpose of saving time, as you spend too much effort crafting the prompt
options.explanation: The article warns that over‑engineering wastes time, which negates AI’s efficiency benefit.

options.text: Your Notion workspace will become corrupted
options.explanation: There’s no risk of workspace corruption from complex prompts.

options.text: The AI will generate content in a different language than requested
options.explanation: Language mismatch isn’t mentioned as a result of over‑engineering.

questions.question: Which of the following templates is NOT explicitly mentioned as being available for daily life use with Notion AI?

options.text: Meal prep template
options.explanation: Meal prep templates are highlighted for AI‑generated recipes.

options.text: Travel planning template
options.explanation: Travel planning is discussed with custom AI blocks.

options.text: SMART goals tracking template
options.explanation: SMART goals tracking is listed among AI‑powered templates.

options.text: Financial investment portfolio template
options.explanation: The guide never mentions a financial investment portfolio template.
